 Gaultheria is a type of flowering plant, specifically a member of the heath family (Ericaceae), characterized by its small, white, bell-shaped flowers and red or purple berries. The health benefits of Gaultheria include its potential use as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and soothe skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Gaultheria include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Gaultheria include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
